prevalence.value: common seasonal illness; high infant burden
prevalence.source: Pediatric respiratory infection syntheses / WHO context
prevalence.trend: seasonal peaks; severe disease concentrated in young infants

burden.dalyPerCapita: high per-case in severe disease; large national infant burden
burden.mortalityRate: Higher than high-income settings; driven by prematurity and limited oxygen access in some areas

vulnerablePopulations: preterm and low birth weight infants
rural households with limited hospital access
epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Clinical diagnosis in infants with wheeze and respiratory distress; pulse oximetry; RSV testing limited

publicHealthSystem.controlRate: Variable; depends on timely oxygen support and referral capacity

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Mostly clinical diagnosis; limited RSV testing outside tertiary centers
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Supportive care with oxygen and hydration; referral for severe cases; avoid unnecessary antibiotics and bronchodilators
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Oxygen availability and referral delays during seasonal surges, especially in rural districts
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Expanding pediatric care networks (SNCU/PHC) and increasing pulse oximetry adoption
